About Zegona
Zegona is publicly listed on the Main Market of the LSE. It was established in 2015 with the objective of investing in businesses in the European Telecommunications, Media and Technology sector and improving their performance to deliver attractive shareholder returns. Zegona is led by former Virgin Media executives Eamonn O'Hare and Robert Samuelson. In 2024, Zegona completed the acquisition of Vodafone Spain.?

latest.title: Directorate change
latest.content:
Today 07:00
RNS Number : 0263V
Zegona Communications PLC
03 March 2026
3 March 2026
LEI: 213800ASI1VZL2ED4S65
Zegona Communications plc
Appointment of Independent
Non-Executive Director
Zegona
is pleased to announce the appointment of Tim Pennington as an Independent Non-Executive Director, effective immediately. He will join the Audit Committee upon appointment and will become Chair of the Audit Committee after the end of this financial year.
T
im is an experienced finance executive with a strong background in corporate finance, telecommunications and mergers and acquisitions.
He currently holds non-executive roles with MTN
Group
Limited
, Africa
's largest
mobile
network
operator
and one of its listed subsidiaries
.
Tim has
held senior financ
e
leadership roles at major
international
telecommunications companies, including Chief Financial Officer of Millicom
International Cellular S.A.
and
Cable & Wireless Communications plc, and
he
was
Group Finance Director for Cable & Wireless
PLC
. Earlier in his career, Tim served as Chief Financial Officer of Hutchison Telecom International and Finance Director at Hutchison 3G (UK), the British mobile business of Hutchison Whampoa. He also bri
ngs investment banking experience from his time as a director at Samuel Montagu & Co. Limited and as Managing Director at HSBC Investment Bank within its Corporate Finance and Advisory Department.
Eamonn O'Hare, Chairman and CEO of Zegona, commented: "On behalf of the Board, I am delighted to welcome Tim to Zegona. His deep understanding of corporate finance and the global telecoms industry will be a great asset to our Board as we continue to execute our strategy and drive value creation."
For further information please contact:
Investor enquiries:
Media enquiries:
Alfonso Enríquez
Jaime De Andres
Tilly Abraham (Sodali & Co)
info
@zegona.com
jaime.andres@vodafone.com
zegona@info.sodali.com
About Zegona
Zegona is publicly listed on the Main Market of the LSE. It was established in 2015 with the objective of investing in businesses in the European Telecommunications, Media and Technology sector and improving their performance to deliver attractive shareholder returns. Zegona is led by former Virgin Media executives Eamonn O'Hare and Robert Samuelson. In 2024, Zegona completed the acquisition of Vodafone Spain.?
This announcement is made pursuant to UKLR 6.4.8. The Company confirms that there are no additional matters requiring disclosure under UKLR 6.4.8.
At the date of this announcement, Tim Pennington has no beneficial interest in the ordinary shares of Zegona.

company_30s.what_they_do: Zegona Ltd. provides high‑speed fiber connectivity across the UK, offering wholesale and retail services to businesses and households. It builds and maintains fiber‑optic networks, leases capacity, and sells broadband packages.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The UK is still far from full fiber coverage, and demand for gigabit connectivity is rising as remote work and cloud services expand. Zegona sits in a niche where infrastructure costs are high but the long‑term revenue stream is stable, giving it a defensible position in a market that is still catching up.
company_30s.current_state: The company has recently been added to the MSCI UK Index, signalling institutional confidence. It is paying a dividend and has just cleared a major EU‑approved fiber transaction. The share price is trading at a very low level, near its 52‑week floor, reflecting a steep discount to its long‑term fundamentals.

rns_narrative: Zegona has secured EU clearance for a large fiber project (FiberPass), which should unlock new revenue streams and improve cash flow.
The company is distributing a dividend and has moved into an index, which increases visibility and may attract passive investors.
Corporate changes – a new directorate, share buyback transactions, and updated voting rights – signal management’s intent to tighten control and create shareholder value.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism: investors are waiting for the dividend and the new fiber deal to spark a bounce.
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Some traders are excited about the upcoming dividend and the fact that the firm is now part of a major index, implying better liquidity and institutional interest.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Others worry that the company’s thin trading volume and the lack of recent earnings make it vulnerable to further price erosion if the fiber deal stalls.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: Sentiment has moved from neutral to mildly bullish as the ex‑dividend date approaches and the FiberPass approval becomes a reality.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends shows no recent search activity, meaning the market is largely unaware of Zegona – a perfect set‑up for a surprise move.

near_term_catalysts: The ex‑dividend date on 9 January – a natural catalyst that often lifts shares as investors buy to capture the payout.
Any announcement of new fiber contracts or revenue‑growth targets following the EU‑approved FiberPass transaction.
A surge in trading volume that exceeds 200 % of the 20‑day average, indicating institutional interest.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a rebound that pushes the price back above the 200‑day moving average and a sustained volume spike. A clear breakout above the recent resistance at the 52‑week low would confirm the mean‑reversion thesis.
expect_next.timing_regime: Zegona appears to be in the early stages of a recovery from a deep bottom. The APEX score indicates a tight compression, so a breakout is likely to be swift. The key is to confirm that the market has fully capitulated before the price starts to climb.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: Zegona has just cleared a large fiber project and is paying a dividend, both of which are strong drivers of demand. The stock sits at a steep discount, and a 50 % recovery to the pre‑crash midpoint would generate a 100 % return. Historically, deep crash signals (70‑95 % drop) have produced the largest outliers, so a bounce to the 52‑week high is plausible.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: The company’s thin trading volume and the absence of recent earnings data could keep the price suppressed if the fiber project stalls. The worst realistic outcome is a further 20 % decline before the dividend is paid, leaving the share at a near‑zero valuation.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential upside: 100 % (price back to 50 % of pre‑crash level). Potential downside: 20 % (additional drop before the dividend). The reward‑to‑risk ratio is roughly 5:1, a classic asymmetric play.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter after the ex‑dividend date if the price closes above the 200‑day moving average and volume exceeds the 20‑day average by 50 %. Size the position to 5 % of your equity and set a stop 10 % below the recent low to protect against a temporary retracement.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until you see a volume spike of at least 200 % of the 20‑day average and the price breaks the 52‑week low resistance by at least 2 %. That will be the signal that the market has accepted the new valuation.
playbook.if_passing: If the company announces a regulatory denial of the FiberPass transaction or a significant downgrade by a major rating agency, consider exiting. No bankruptcy or delisting risk has surfaced yet, so the default stance remains bullish.

ai_insights.bottom_line: Zegona’s share sits at a deep bottom, the dividend and FiberPass approval are poised to ignite a rebound, and the upside far outweighs the limited downside, making a contrarian bullish bet a compelling play.
